mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals continued labor market expansion that supports household income and consumption, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the positive return rate in the top PAYEMS-growth quintile fails to exceed that of the bottom quintile (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 2.0 percent indicates sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that raises corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate signal an aggressive central bank response to price pressures that rapidly tightens financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change positive and FEDFUNDS 3-month change positive
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS signal remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, further declines signal an overheating labor market that raises wage-push inflation expectations and impending Federal Reserve tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with unemployment below 4 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations when UNRATE is below 4.0 percent and declining.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production of more than 2 percent over three months signals a contracting manufacturing sector that reduces corporate earnings and investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 positive 60-day returns occur in more than 45 percent of instances when INDPRO declines by more than 2 percent over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand strength across consumer and residential investment sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month change positive and HOUST 1-month change positive
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint RSAFS-HOUST signal remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5 percent year-over-year, month-over-month increases in retail sales signal demand-driven inflation pressure that prompts tighter monetary policy and equity risk re-pricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5 percent and RSAFS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates a contracting labor market that immediately reduces consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 positive 5-day returns occur in more than 45 percent of months when PAYEMS declines month-over-month.
